behavior: Primarily a foliage gleaner; uses its stout bill for seeds, fruit, and insects, and may hover or fly out to catch insects.
field_marks: Stocky, broad-chested songbird with a short neck and medium-length squared tail.
Very large, thick triangular bill, pale horn-colored in the adult male.
Adult male black above with crisp white wing patches and white underparts.
Adult male has a vivid rose-red breast chevron below the black throat.
White patches show in the wings and tail; males show pink-red underwings in flight.
Female and immature are brown and heavily streaked, with a bold pale eyebrow and pale bill.
habitat: Moist deciduous or mixed woodland, especially second-growth woods, thickets, and shrubby forest edges; also uses semiopen wooded habitats.
